title: Oneworld Alliance, Airlines and Breakthrough Energy Ventures Launch Investment Fund to Advance and Commercialize Sustainable Aviation Fuel Technologies
content:
The oneworld alliance and its member airlines have partnered with Breakthrough Energy Ventures (BEV) to launch a new investment fund aimed at addressing the limited availability and high cost of Sustainable Aviation Fuels (SAF). The fund, which has an initial close of $150 million, is led by cornerstone investors Alaska Airlines and American Airlines, with participation from IAG, Cathay Pacific, Japan Airlines, and non-alliance member Singapore Airlines.

The oneworld BEV Fund will invest in next-generation SAF technologies, support the growth of alternative fuel markets, and drive technology innovation to create a more diverse and resilient SAF supply chain. This initiative is a strategic effort to find long-term solutions that are cost-effective, scalable, and have lower emissions than conventional fuels.

According to oneworld Chairman Robert Isom, the investment is a "business decision" to unlock novel technologies that can be produced at a larger scale for lower prices. The fund's managers at BEV will use their technical expertise to identify and scale breakthrough SAF technologies that can deliver real emissions reductions, compete with fossil fuels on cost, and seamlessly integrate into existing aviation infrastructure.
author: Analysts Account
